name: Dilly Casserole Bread (1960)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift 2 cups all-purpose flour into a large bowl and stir.
2. In a separate bowl, whisk together granulated sugar, onion powder, instant minced onion, dill seeds, salt, baking soda, and yeast with water until well combined.
3. Gradually add the wet mixture to the dry ingredients, stirring until just combined.
4. Pour in the creamed cottage cheese and egg; beat until a smooth batter forms.
5. Gently fold in the remaining 1 cup of flour using a spatula until all ingredients are incorporated.
6. Cover the bowl with greased plastic wrap and a cloth towel. Let rise in a warm place (80-85°F) until doubled in size, about 45 to 60 minutes.
7. Preheat oven to 350°F.
8. Grease a 1 1/2 or 2-quart casserole dish.
9. Gently turn the dough into the prepared casserole dish and cover with plastic wrap and towel.
10. Let rise in a warm place until doubled in size, about 30 to 45 minutes.
11. Remove from the casserole dish and transfer to oven.
12. Bake for 30 to 40 minutes or until golden brown and sounds hollow when tapped lightly.
13. Brush with melted butter and sprinkle with coarse salt before serving.
